name: Grievance Redressal Procedure
text: The Employer shall maintain a grievance redressal mechanism for the resolution of employee grievances. Any Employee who has a grievance relating to the conditions of service, working environment, conduct of colleagues, or any other employment-related matter shall follow the following procedure: (a) raise the grievance in writing with the immediate supervisor within thirty (30) days of the event giving rise to the grievance; (b) if unresolved within seven (7) days, escalate the grievance in writing to the HR Department; (c) if still unresolved within fifteen (15) days of escalation, the Employee may request a formal grievance hearing before a grievance committee constituted by the Employer. The Employer shall ensure that all grievances are addressed promptly, fairly, and without victimisation of the Employee. For grievances relating to sexual harassment, the Employer shall follow the procedure prescribed under the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013.

legal_basis.note: Grievance redressal committees in establishments with 20+ workers

legal_basis.act: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013
legal_basis.note: Internal Complaints Committee — mandatory for 10+ employees

invalid_if: No grievance mechanism for sexual harassment complaints as mandated by POSH Act 2013
source: Industrial Disputes Act, 1947 S.9C; POSH Act, 2013
title: Grievance Redressal Procedure
dispute_hotspots: POSH Act non-compliance — no ICC — employer faces Rs.50,000 fine and licence cancellation under S.26
ICC composition — requires external member; without external member, entire proceedings may be void
